items.title: Pakistan urges restraint, calls for revival of Iran-US talks under Islamabad MoU
items.summary:
Pakistan on Thursday renewed its call for restraint and dialogue amid continuing tensions involving Iran and the United States, warning that further escalation would undermine regional peace and stability and adversely affect global energy supplies and trade.
Addressing his weekly press briefing in Islamabad, Foreign Office Spokesperson Tahir Andrabi said Pakistan had expressed deep concern over t

items.title: US military says it completed latest strikes on Iran, targets included Bandar Abbas
items.summary:
WASHINGTON: The US military said late on Wednesday ​it completed its latest wave of strikes on Iran that it carried ‌out at President Donald Trump’s direction, with targets including Bandar Abbas, Iran’s principal port city on the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: 'Heartstopper Forever' cast bids 'bittersweet' farewell to Netflix franchise
items.summary:
LONDON: “Heartstopper Forever” signals a major shift towards adult territory for the franchise, its star Joe Locke says, as the hit Netflix coming-of-age drama comes to ​a close.

items.title: Will Ferrell reunites with longtime collaborators for Netflix golf comedy ‘The Hawk’
items.summary:
LOS ANGELES: For actor Will Ferrell, assembling a cast filled with familiar faces for the Netflix golf comedy series, “The Hawk,” added a special chemistry ​that elevated the show.
The “Step Brothers” actor recalled many shared laughs that went ‌beyond the humor of the series.

items.title: US House defeats bid to cut off Israel aid in vote dividing Democrats
items.summary:
WASHINGTON: The US House of Representatives defeated an amendment to cut off aid to Israel on Wednesday, despite nearly half of Democrats ​supporting it, reflecting a growing rupture between U.S. progressives and Israel over its attacks in Gaza.
The House voted 314 to ‌104 to defeat the measure, offered as an amendment to a State Department spending bill by Republican Representative Thomas Massie

items.title: DPM Dar departs for China to participate in World AI Cooperation Organization
items.summary:
Deputy Prime Minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar on Thursday departed for Shanghai, China, on a two-day visit.
As Pakistan joins the World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organization (WAICO) as a founding member, the deputy prime minister will sign the agreement, participate in the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference and engage with leaders to advance international AI cooperation and promot

items.title: South African rand under pressure as investors monitor latest US strikes on Iran
items.summary:
JOHANNESBURG: The South African rand came under pressure on Thursday, as the latest escalation in hostilities between the United States and Iran pushed oil prices back up and reignited inflation concerns.  
At 0635 GMT the rand traded at 16.35 against the dollar , near its previous close as investors held back from making big bets.
The US struck Iran’s coastal defences and missile sites on Wednesd

items.title: Russia says it hits military targets in Kyiv, Ukrainian ports
items.summary:
MOSCOW: Russia’s defence ministry said on Thursday it hit military and industrial facilities in the Ukrainian capital of Kyiv, as well as infrastructure at ports in Odesa and Pivdennyi and a maritime vessel.
Both Moscow and Kyiv have been stepping up their attacks on key economic targets, with Ukrainian forces hitting Russian energy infrastructure, including oil tankers, while Russia has intensifi

items.title: Thai baht/US dollar weaker on Thursday
items.summary:
BANGKOK: The Thai baht weakened against the US dollar on Thursday.
At 0155 GMT, the baht was 0.13% lower at 33.59 versus the dollar, after trading in a range of 33.560 to 33.630.
It ended the previous session at 33.54 per dollar, as per LSEG data.

items.title: Japan bond yields rise as US-Iran tensions fuel inflation concerns
items.summary:
TOKYO: Japanese government bond (JGB) yields rose on Thursday as escalating US-Iran tensions drove oil prices higher, fuelling inflation concerns and adding to persistent worries about Japan’s fiscal health.  
Here are a few details:
The benchmark 10-year JGB yield rose 0.5 basis point (bp) to 2.690%. Yields move inversely to bond prices.
Oil prices rose for a fourth consecutive session after a ne

items.title: Rubio, Wang Yi to attend ASEAN meetings in Philippines
items.summary: MANILA: The Philippine foreign ministry said on Thursday it has information that US State Secretary Marco Rubio will attend next week’s ASEAN and related meetings in Manila, as will his Chinese counterpart Wang Yi Foreign ministry spokesperson Dominic Xavier Imperial made the remarks at a briefing.

items.title: England's pattern of World Cup knockout elimination against top sides repeats — and wait for glory continues
items.summary:
England led. England lost. England went home. Again.
Anthony Gordon’s 55th-minute goal had the Three Lions dreaming. For 30 minutes, they were the better side, disciplined and dangerous, with one foot in a first World Cup final since 1966. Then came the familiar collapse.

items.title: Iran says Hormuz is a red line, warns of attacks on regional infrastructure if US strikes Iran
items.summary:
DUBAI: The spokesperson for Iran’s top military command said Tehran would not allow US interference in the Strait of Hormuz, calling it a red line.
Iran would target infrastructure across the region if US President Donald Trump carried out threats to attack Iranian infrastructure, the spokesperson warned.

items.title: UK regulator to probe TikTok on child safety measures
items.summary:
Britain’s media regulator launched an investigation into TikTok on Thursday to understand whether its UK unit failed or is failing to protect children from harmful content.
A month ago, the government imposed a blanket ban on social media for users under the age of 16 and placed restrictions on gaming and live-streaming platforms.  
Here are some more details on the investigation:
Ofcom will look

items.title: Wahdat Poultry announces expansion plans
items.summary:
Wahdat Poultry Farm Limited has approved its annual budget and business plan for the financial year ending June 30, 2027, while also giving the green light to a series of strategic expansion initiatives aimed at strengthening its value-added product portfolio and operational efficiency.
The decisions were taken at a meeting of the company’s Board of Directors held on Wednesday in Islamabad, as per

items.title: Australian stocks inch lower; BHP falls on weaker copper output
items.summary:
Australian shares edged lower on Thursday as losses in mining and energy stocks outweighed gains in financials, with BHP dragging on the benchmark after weaker quarterly copper output.
The S&P/ASX 200 index fell 0.1% to 8,831.80 by 0006 GMT after a 0.4% gain on Wednesday.
BHP Group shares dropped 0.7% after the world’s largest listed miner posted a 5% fall in copper production due to lower con

items.title: India bonds could gain at open as Treasury yields soften further
items.summary:
MUMBAI: Indian government bonds are likely to rise marginally in early deals on Thursday, tracking declines in Treasury yields and as oil prices stay flattish despite escalating hostilities in the Middle East.
The benchmark 6.94% 2036 bond yield is expected to move between 6.74% and 6.78%, a trader at a private bank said. It ended the previous session at 6.7738%.
Bond yields move inversely to pric

items.title: ABB beats forecasts with Q2 earnings and announces biggest-ever acquisition
items.summary:
ZURICH: ABB reported better-than-expected second quarter earnings on Thursday as the Swiss engineering company also announced its biggest-ever acquisition with a $5.5 billion deal for British automation company Rotork.
ABB said its operational earnings before interest, tax and amortisation (EBITA) rose 20% to $1.93 billion in the three months to the end of June.

items.title: PSX continues recovery as KSE-100 index gains 2,600 points in early trade
items.summary:
The Pakistan Stock Exchange’s (PSX) benchmark index continued to recover on Thursday as it surged by around 2,600 points during early trade.
The KSE-100 index gained 2,604.49 points (1.49 per cent) by 11:04am to stand at 177,890.27 points from the previous close of 175,285.78.
Awais Ashraf, director of research at AKD Securities, noted that the market continued Wednesday’s rebound after US Preside

items.title: Norway's Telenor lags second-quarter core earnings expectations
items.summary:
Norway’s leading telecom operator Telenor reported second-quarter earnings below analysts’ expectations on Thursday, citing tough year-on-year comparisons, increased infrastructure hardening and transformation costs, and macroeconomic challenges in Bangladesh.
Telenor-Ufone merger may trigger job cuts

items.title: Flash flood emergency strikes Texas year after Camp Mystic disaster
items.summary:
A flash flood watch was posted across a broad swath of Texas on Wednesday as heavy downpours turned rivers into raging, life-threatening torrents and washed out more than 100 roads, leading to dozens of rescues of people stranded by high water.
The hardest hit region, according to Texas Governor Greg Abbott, was the Nueces River basin, which runs from a plateau northwest of San Antonio south throu

items.title: World Cup's 'Chief Watchers' savour tournament's dream job
items.summary:
NEW YORK: World Cup fanatics Kevin Akoto and Austin Franklin have lived the dream of any diehard football fan this year: watching every match from the tournament from start to finish - and getting paid for it.
The “Chief World Cup Watchers” for US broadcaster Fox One will add a unique item to their resumes when the final whistle sounds in Sunday’s final between Spain and Argentina, having watched

items.title: More than 500 feared dead after boats sink off Myanmar coast, UN says
items.summary:
BANGKOK: Two boats carrying more than 500 people may have capsized off the coast of Myanmar in recent days, UN agencies said on Thursday, as refugees from the war-torn country continue to make perilous maritime journeys in search of safety and better opportunities.
“According to preliminary information, the two vessels departed from Myanmar’s Rakhine State in late June carrying mostly Rohingya pas

items.title: Nickel rises on concerns over supply via Hormuz
items.summary:
SINGAPORE: Nickel rose nearly 2% on Thursday as concern over disruptions to traffic through the Strait of Hormuz raised fears of sulphur supply shortages, threatening a key raw material for production.
Benchmark three-month nickel on the London Metal Exchange climbed 1.83% to $17,110 a metric ton by 0300 GMT.
The most-traded nickel contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange was up 2.01% at 131,640

items.title: Outgoing UK PM Starmer to visit Kyiv for talks with Zelenskiy
items.summary:
Britain’s outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer will travel to Kyiv on Thursday for talks with President Volodymyr Zelenskiy in one of his final visits as UK leader, Downing Street said.
Starmer, who is due to step down on Monday, will discuss progress made in equipping Ukraine in its fight against Russia’s invasion and what allies should focus on next, Downing Street said in a statement.
Andy Burn

items.title: Japan's Nikkei drops over 3% as chip stocks slide
items.summary:
TOKYO: Japan’s Nikkei share average dropped more than 3% on Thursday, weighed down by chip-related stocks, as investors awaited results from Taiwan’s TSMC after markets had largely priced in a solid outlook from Dutch chip equipment maker ASML.
The Nikkei lost 3.1% to 66612.63 in late-morning Asian trading.
The broader Topix slid 1.2% to 4,039.56.

items.title: Nvidia partners with Japan robotics firms on AI development
items.summary:
TOKYO: Nvidia said on Thursday it was partnering with Japanese companies including Fanuc and Yaskawa Electric to advance the development of robotics and AI.
“With AI, robots will become smart, easily adaptable and accessible,” Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said at a media event in Tokyo.
On Wednesday Huang attended an event held by gaming firm Sega Sammy in the Akihabara electronics district and ate din

items.title: China's yuan pulls back from one-month high as tensions flare in Middle East
items.summary:
SHANGHAI: China’s yuan pulled back from a one-month high against the dollar on Thursday, weighed down by renewed US-Iran tensions, although broad greenback weakness and reduced expectations for domestic easing lent support.
Escalating hostilities between the Washington and Tehran kept oil prices near one-month highs, while the dollar hovered near a one-month low after cooling US producer price dat

items.title: Palm falls for second day, tracks weakness in Dalian and Chicago edible oils
items.summary:
JAKARTA: Malaysian palm oil futures inched lower for a second session on Thursday, pressured by weakness in Chicago and Dalian edible oils and a stronger Malaysian ringgit.
The benchmark palm oil contract for October delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange lost 42 ringgit, or 0.52%, to 4,577 ringgit ($1,124.29) a metric ton by 0240 GMT.

items.title: China shares fall on regional chip rout; Alibaba lifts Hong Kong
items.summary:
SHANGHAI: Mainland China stocks fell on Thursday, weighed by weakness in tech shares following a sell-off among their regional peers, and Alibaba boosted Hong Kong shares, while diminishing expectations of an imminent Federal Reserve rate hike lent support. 
At the midday break, the benchmark Shanghai Composite index slipped 0.8%, while the blue-chip CSI300 index lost 0.9%.

items.title: South Korean shares tumble nearly 8% on chipmaker selloff, won steady after rate hike
items.summary:
South Korean shares tumbled nearly 8% on Thursday on renewed selling in chipmakers, while the won held near a two-month high after the central bank raised interest rates for the first time in more than three years and signalled more tightening.
The benchmark KOSPI fell as much as 7.6% to 6,730.87 points, erasing the previous session’s gains.
Sharp declines also triggered sidecar trading curbs on t

items.title: US imposes 25% tariff on some imports from Brazil
items.summary:
The US plans to impose a 25% tariff on certain imports from Brazil under Section 301 of the Trade Act, US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said late on Wednesday.

items.title: Pink salt value addition finance scheme launched in Punjab
items.summary:
LAHORE: Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz on Wednesday digitally inaugurated the Pink Salt Value Addition Financing Scheme, aimed at promoting industry, employment and exports through value addition to the province’s pink salt reserves.

The chief minister also announced the establishment of a 110-acre Pink Salt Mineral Processing Zone near Quaidabad.

items.title: Large-Scale Manufacturing grows 5.77pc despite May slump
items.summary:
ISLAMABAD: The Large-Scale Manufacturing (LSM) sector posted a year-on-year growth of 5.77 per cent in the first 11 months of 2025-26, suggesting a revival in industrial production.

items.title: Agriculture can revive economy quickly: PM Shehbaz
items.summary:
ISLAMABAD: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Wednesday said Pakistan could revive its economy within a year by focusing on the agriculture and livestock sectors, urging rapid adoption of modern technology and greater value addition to capitalise on the country’s untapped potential.

“Agriculture and livestock constitute the single sector where, if we work with complete, undivided focus, we can revi

items.title: Textile exports stagnate at $17.9bn in FY26
items.summary:
ISLAMABAD: Pakis­tan’s textile and clothing exports posted a marginal growth of 0.26 per cent in FY26, reflecting the sector’s struggle to maintain momentum amid rising production costs and weakening demand in key regional markets.

The increase, one of the weakest performances in recent years, was attributed to elevated input costs, which eroded exporters’ competitiveness, and to external shocks

items.title: NFC share declined after aiding Centre, says Sindh chief minister
items.summary:
KARACHI: Sindh Chief Minister Murad Ali Shah on Wednesday said the amount allocated to provinces under the National Finance Commission (NFC) Award had declined this year because the provinces contributed a portion of their share to support the federal government.

items.summary:
Pakistan Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (PVARA) Chairman Bilal bin Saqib has asked Jamia Darul Uloom to draw a distinction between speculative cryptocurrencies and asset-backed digital tokens, its chief said on Wednesday.
The crypto czar told Reuters he had asked for the clarification after the seminary ruled last month that crypto-based purchases were not permissible under Islamic law.

items.title: ADB formally appointed financial, transaction adviser for outsourcing Islamabad Airport operations
items.summary:
ISLAMABAD: Pakistan on Wednesday formally appointed the Asian Development Bank (ADB) as the financial and transaction adviser for the outsourcing of Islamabad International Airport’s operations.
Last week, the Privatisation Commission Board reviewed the terms of a transaction advisory services agreement (TASA) with the lender and gave its approval.

items.title: Bulls snap losing streak as PSX gains over 1,700 points
items.summary:
The Pakistan Stock Exchange’s (PSX) benchmark index snapped a two-day losing streak on Wednesday, gaining more than 1,700 points to settle above the 175,000 mark.
The KSE-100 index rose by 1,766.97 points (1.02 per cent) over the course of the day after a volatile session. It closed at 175,285.78 points, up from its previous close of 173,518.81.
